title: Milan NIC device-tree intermediate representation (IR)
description: Platform-neutral description of where the Milan TSN NIC (kl,dma-ether) lives on one SoC. An extractor produces this from a platform (LiteX csr.json, a Zynq export, ...); the generator (sw/dts/milan_dt.py gen) renders the kl,dma-ether node from it. The invariant node contract (compatible, reg-names/interrupt-names ordering, property names) lives in the generator, NOT here.

address_cells.description: #address-cells / #size-cells the overlay's parent uses. 1 = 32-bit reg (all current platforms); 2 = 64-bit, splits each base/size into hi/lo cells.

interrupt_parent.description: Label of the SoC interrupt controller in the base tree ('intc0' for a LiteX PLIC, 'intc' for the Zynq GIC). null => the node is placed under a parent that already sets interrupt-parent (inherit).

reg.description: Register windows keyed by binding reg-name; the generator emits reg/reg-names in canonical order (csr, dma-tx, dma-rx, dma-ts), present keys only.

interrupts.description: The NIC's interrupt line(s). LiteX exposes ONE aggregate line (EventManager-muxed, name 'csr'); Zynq exposes four (tx-dma/rx-dma/ts-dma/csr). The generator emits interrupts + interrupt-names from this list.

cells.description: raw interrupt-specifier cells for controllers with #interrupt-cells>1, e.g. a Zynq GIC SPI line [0, 29, 4].

base.description: byte base address (int, or a hex string like '0x40410000').
